text.title: client-conventions-reverse-engineering Worked Examples
text.summary: One full end-to-end example of client-conventions-reverse-engineering applied to a representative input.

section.title: Worked example
section.p: Client: github.com/acme/web. Stack: TypeScript + Next.js. Lint: ESLint with airbnb base + custom overrides. Sample: 24 merged PRs over 30 days, 6 authors.

example.code:
# Repo Rules — github.com/acme/web
## Lint / formatter - Tool: eslint + prettier - Config: .eslintrc.json (airbnb base + 14 overrides) - Note: no-default-export is error; import/order is error.
## Naming conventions - Files: kebab-case (.ts/.tsx) - Classes: PascalCase - Functions: camelCase - React components: PascalCase file + named export
## Branching + commit - Branch model: github-flow (short-lived from main) - Commit format: conventional (feat:, fix:, chore:)
## Dependency policy - Updates: renovate, weekly batch PR - Allowed sources: public npm + @acme scope
## Layering rules - src/app/ may import src/lib/, never the reverse. - No fetch in src/components/; use src/lib/api/ wrappers.
## Test placement - Unit: colocated *.test.ts - E2E: tests/e2e/
example.note: Each section cites either the eslint config path or specific PR numbers (omitted in this snippet for brevity).
